scope_note: Northwestern does not publish a minimum GPA cutoff, an admitted-student GPA distribution, or any GPA breakdown by major. Their cited admissions materials frame academic review in context rather than around a numeric threshold.
policy_summary: Northwestern does not state a minimum required GPA on its cited admissions page. Instead, Northwestern asks counselors to contextualize a student’s curriculum and invites applicants to explain circumstances that significantly affected academic performance.

what_mit_does_say.claim: Northwestern does not publish a minimum GPA cutoff for first-year applicants on its cited admissions page.
Northwestern asks the counselor recommendation to speak to the student’s overall high school curriculum and involvement within the context of that high school.
Northwestern explicitly invites applicants to use the Additional Information section to explain circumstances that significantly affected academic performance.

recommended_gpa.summary: Northwestern does not publish HS GPA data for its incoming class. The numbers below are third-party estimates, not official Northwestern figures. Use them as directional references, not cutoffs.

estimates.label: Weighted (estimate)
estimates.method: Estimated using data from over 1,000 schools, because Northwestern did not officially report its average GPA.

estimates.label: Unweighted (prediction)
estimates.method: Predicted by a CollegeSimply algorithm using Northwestern's test scores and a large sample of college GPA data, because Northwestern has not published HS GPA data for incoming freshmen.

recommended_gpa.interpretation: Both estimates land in the near-straight-A range. The weighted 4.1 implies a highly rigorous schedule with top marks, while the unweighted 3.92 points to an A/A- level transcript.
